signals.headline: Market Implications**
signals.raw:
Market Implications**

Despite inflation concerns, the **S&P 500 hit an all-time high of 7,041.28** after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz open to commercial traffic, suggesting Middle East tensions may be easing[2]. However, the persistent inflation data and delayed rate cuts represent headwinds for bonds and rate-sensitive sectors, while benefiting energy stocks.
